desc.cn: 首都機場落地。免費 Wi-Fi 要國內手機號收驗證碼。你與外界完全失聯，行李轉盤還在轉。
desc.en: Landed at Capital Airport. Free Wi-Fi needs a Chinese number for SMS. You are offline while baggage keeps spinning.

text.cn: 排隊辦預付卡（護照實名），忍痛買流量。
text.en: Queue for a prepaid SIM (passport real-name). Pay for data.

feedback.cn: 花了 150 元和 1.5 小時。你終於有中國號了——後面綁 App 會絲滑很多。
feedback.en: 150 RMB and 1.5 hours later, you have a Chinese number. App binding will be smoother.

text.cn: 拒絕辦卡，靠國際漫遊硬撐（無國內號）。
text.en: Skip the SIM. Survive on international roaming (no local number).

feedback.cn: 省了錢，但之後凡是要簡訊驗證的場景，都會變成噩夢。
feedback.en: You saved money. Every SMS-verification scene ahead will be a nightmare.

desc.cn: 肚子空了。便利店貼著：『不收大額外幣，建議掃碼。』你必須立刻決定要不要綁移動支付。
desc.en: You're starving. The store sign says: no large foreign cash — scan to pay. Decide on mobile payment now.

text.cn: 下載支付寶/微信，上傳護照綁境外卡。
text.en: Install Alipay/WeChat Pay, upload passport, link a foreign card.

feedback.cn: 折騰一小時、護照傳兩次，終於綁上。之後大額可能吃 3% 外卡費。
feedback.en: One hour and two passport uploads later, it works. Big spends may take a 3% fee.

text.cn: 拒絕上傳隱私，堅持人民幣現金硬闖。
text.en: Refuse ID uploads. Survive on RMB cash only.

feedback.cn: 店員找零找到懷疑人生。你隱約預感到：上海、深圳會更残酷。
feedback.en: Making change becomes a negotiation. Shanghai and Shenzhen will be crueler.

desc.cn: 酒店在城區，離機場出奇的遠。夜色漸深，你要選一條能活著抵達的路。
desc.en: Your hotel is deep in the city — surprisingly far. Night falls. Pick a way that gets you there alive.

text.cn: 坐機場快軌 + 地鐵換乘（便宜，但行李拖到懷疑人生）。
text.en: Airport Express + metro transfers (cheap; luggage will hate you).

feedback.cn: 行李在閘機卡了兩次。你學會了『先看進出站箭頭』這門北京必修課。
feedback.en: Luggage jams the gate twice. You learn Beijing's mandatory lesson: watch the entry/exit arrows.

text.cn: 坐機場大巴到市區站點，再步行/打車最後一公里。
text.en: Airport bus to a city stop, then walk/taxi the last mile.

feedback.cn: 大巴準點，但『市區站』離你訂的酒店還有四站地鐵的心理距離。
feedback.en: The bus is on time — the 'city stop' is still a mental four metro stops from your hotel.

text.cn: 網約車/計程車直達（貴、快，看你有沒有號與支付）。
text.en: Ride-hail/taxi direct (pricey, fast — if you have number & payment).

feedback.cn: 司機用中文問目的地，你比劃酒店英文名。導航還算聽話，錢包先哭為敬。
feedback.en: Driver asks in Chinese; you mime the hotel name. Navigation cooperates. Your wallet does not.

desc.cn: 你拖著行李走進評價不錯的經濟型酒店。前台掃一眼護照：『不好意思，我們不能接待境外客人。』——這不是刻板印象，這是政策與系統限制。
desc.en: You drag luggage into a well-reviewed budget hotel. Front desk glances at your passport: 'Sorry, we cannot host foreign guests.' Not a stereotype — policy and system limits.

text.cn: 禮貌理論、出示簽證頁，試圖通融。
text.en: Argue politely, show visa page, try to negotiate.

feedback.cn: 前台打開公安登記系統搖頭：外賓字段是灰的。通融失敗，時間與自尊雙雙扣血。
feedback.en: They open the police registration system — foreigner fields are greyed out. Negotiation fails. Time and dignity take damage.

text.cn: 立刻在路上用手機重搜『可接待外賓』的酒店。
text.en: Search again on the street for hotels that accept foreigners.

feedback.cn: 篩選條件多打了幾個字，結果貴了一截。你開始理解為什麼攻略反覆提醒『外賓可住』。
feedback.en: Extra filters appear. Prices jump. You finally get why guides spam 'foreigner-friendly'.

desc.cn: 便宜的走不了。你必須再掏一筆交通費，搬去更貴、但系統允許登記的酒店。凌晨的北京街頭，行李箱輪子特別響。
desc.en: Budget is closed to you. Pay more transport and move to a pricier hotel that can register foreigners. Beijing at midnight — suitcase wheels are loud.

text.cn: 入住國際連鎖/四星（貴，但護照一掃就過）。
text.en: Check into an international/4-star (expensive; passport scans fine).

feedback.cn: 前台英語流利，登記 3 分鐘結束。你躺上床，第一次覺得『貴有貴的道理』。
feedback.en: Fluent English at reception; check-in in 3 minutes. In bed, you admit expensive sometimes means possible.

text.cn: 找標明可接待外賓的精品旅店（中等價，可能還要押金糾結）。
text.en: Find a boutique that lists 'foreigners OK' (mid price; deposit drama possible).

feedback.cn: 押金要用移動支付預授權。你終於住下，但對『簡單入住』徹底祛魅。
feedback.en: Deposit needs mobile pre-auth. You're in — and fully disillusioned about 'simple check-in'.

text.cn: 臨時找在北京的網友/同事沙發（省錢，但社交電量歸零）。
text.en: Crash on a Beijing contact's couch (cheap; social battery = 0).

feedback.cn: 你欠了一個人情，也學會了北京的夜公車末班時間。
feedback.en: You owe a favor — and learn Beijing's last-bus timetable the hard way.

title.cn: 第六關：北京第一天去哪？

desc.cn: 你總算安頓下來。北京很大，時間有限——選一個『今天就去』的目標。每個選擇都很『北京刻板印象』，也都很真實。
desc.en: You're settled. Beijing is huge and time is finite — pick today's target. Each option is a stereotype… because it's real.

text.cn: 衝故宮：預約地獄 + 安檢長隊（耗時巨獸）。
text.en: Forbidden City: reservation hell + security lines (time monster).

feedback.cn: 護照格式報錯三次後你進了宮。太和殿很壯觀，腿比精神更先投降。
feedback.en: Passport format fails thrice, then you're in. The halls are grand; your legs surrender before your mind.

text.cn: 衝長城（八達嶺/慕田峪）：車程長、人擠、腿軟。
text.en: Great Wall (Badaling/Mutianyu): long ride, crowds, jelly legs.

feedback.cn: 『不到長城非好漢』——你成為好漢的同時，也成為了防曬與瓶裝水的奴隸。
feedback.en: 'You're not a hero until the Wall' — you become a hero and a slave to sunscreen and bottled water.

text.cn: 南鑼鼓巷/什剎海：網紅街 + 租金傳説 + 咖啡。
text.en: Nanluoguxiang / Shichahai: influencer streets, rent myths, coffee.

feedback.cn: 你買了一個可能在別處更便宜的souvenir，但胡同光影確實好看。
feedback.en: You buy a souvenir that is probably cheaper elsewhere — the hutong light is still worth it.

text.cn: 天壇：廣場空曠、太極大爺、相對『可呼吸』。
text.en: Temple of Heaven: open plazas, tai-chi uncles, relatively breathable.

feedback.cn: 你站在圜丘上發呆。北京不一定總是預約戰爭——有時只是風很大。
feedback.en: You zone out on the Circular Mound. Beijing isn't always reservation war — sometimes it's just windy.

completionReward.logMessage: 新手教程完成。你進入北京景點地圖：每個景點是獨立一天；沙發客需先重新安排住宿。
